summary: Implemented ADR 0252 from an isolated latest-origin/main worktree by reconciling the governed Hetzner DNS drift set, repairing the private Vaultwarden publication path, re-expanding the shared edge certificate for mail.example.com, and re-running the route-and-DNS exposure audit end to end.

verification.check: Focused ADR 0252 regression and live-fix coverage
verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml pytest -q tests/test_proxmox_tailscale_proxy_role.py tests/test_vaultwarden_runtime_role.py` returned `9 passed in 1.93s`, and `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml pytest -q tests/test_subdomain_exposure_audit.py tests/test_proxmox_tailscale_proxy_role.py tests/test_vaultwarden_runtime_role.py` returned `22 passed in 1.35s` after the private-route and short-lived TLS audit fixes.

`make route-dns-assertion-ledger` completed successfully from the isolated worktree with final recap `localhost ok=60 changed=0 failed=0 skipped=20`, leaving `example.com` and `mail.example.com` on `203.0.113.1`, `database.example.com` and `vault.example.com` on `100.64.0.1`, and removing the stale apex `AAAA`, `autoconfig`, `notify`, and `www` records.

verification.check: Vaultwarden private route recovery and replay
verification.observed: `make converge-vaultwarden` completed successfully after the branch fixed Tailscale socket listener detection and Docker bridge/network recovery, with final recap `docker-runtime-lv3 ok=105 changed=5 failed=0 skipped=14 rescued=1`, `postgres-lv3 ok=42 changed=0 failed=0 skipped=7`, and `proxmox-host ok=40 changed=9 failed=0 skipped=13`; `curl --cacert /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/step-ca/certs/root_ca.crt -Is https://vault.example.com/alive` then returned `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`.

verification.check: Shared edge publication and certificate expansion
verification.observed: `make configure-edge-publication` completed successfully with `nginx-lv3 ok=62 changed=5 failed=0 skipped=13`, `curl -I https://mail.example.com` returned `HTTP/2 200`, `curl -I https://example.com` returned `HTTP/2 308` redirecting to `https://nginx.example.com/`, and the live certificate SAN set now includes `DNS:mail.example.com`.

verification.check: Final route and DNS publication audit
verification.observed: `make subdomain-exposure-audit` wrote audit run `d45fbd4e-f07a-4b07-8c94-5ba73cef0298` at `2026-03-29T00:04:34.903617Z`; the ADR 0252 governed drift set passed, while the audit still reported unrelated pre-existing findings for `git.example.com` (`active_subdomain_missing_expected_public_resolution`, `active_catalog_subdomain_missing_from_zone`) and `registry.example.com` (`undeclared_subdomain_present_in_zone`).

notes: During diagnosis, direct Hetzner DNS API PUT and POST requests were used to inspect and partially correct provider state before the final idempotent `make route-dns-assertion-ledger` replay. The committed playbook remains the durable mutation path and the final zone state now matches it.

The successful Vaultwarden replay required two branch fixes discovered during live apply: accept `%tailscale0`-style listener output when verifying proxy sockets, and recover stale Docker bridge or missing compose-network state before retrying `docker compose up`.

The live branch intentionally defers protected integration files to the final mainline step: `VERSION`, the numbered release section in `changelog.md`, the top-level `README.md` status summary, and `versions/stack.yaml` still need the merge-to-main update.

Audit run `d45fbd4e-f07a-4b07-8c94-5ba73cef0298` still reports pre-existing out-of-scope drift for `git.example.com` and `registry.example.com`; ADR 0252 verification for the governed apex, mail, database, and vault routes succeeded.
